typecheck.command: echo "No typecheck configured (no mypy/pyright in project)"

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: Hook scripts and shell scripts must use `$MEMORY_ROOT/.venv/bin/python` (not system `python3`) for any script that imports non-stdlib packages (embed.py, distill.py, inject.py, mcp_server.py). Only ingest.py and entities.py are stdlib-only safe. The established pattern is: PYTHON="$MEMORY_ROOT/.venv/bin/python" at the top of the script.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Round 1 hook-script feature used system python3, causing silent failures for embed.py (numpy) and distill.py (httpx). Fix in round 2 established PYTHON variable pattern in hooks/memory-hook.sh. The convention prevents recurrence in future shell scripts.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add convention: When adding categories/enums to validation sets, also update corresponding docstrings and user-facing documentation strings in the same commit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: memory_add_fact runtime validation set had 8 categories but its docstring only listed 6. The mismatch went unnoticed across two features (mcp-inspect-tool added the validation entries, error-solution-categories was expected to catch it). Fixed in fix-hook-script.

rejectedObservations.observation: The fix establishes a pattern of using PYTHON="$MEMORY_ROOT/.venv/bin/python" in shell scripts. AGENTS.md documents that tests use '.venv/bin/python -m pytest' but doesn't mention the convention for hook/shell scripts.
rejectedObservations.reason: duplicate — already captured in suggestedGuidanceUpdates from round 1, carried forward to round 2 recommendation
